text: Roses are red
Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,And sorry I could not travel bothAnd be one traveler, long I stoodAnd looked down one as far as I couldTo where it bent in the undergrowth;

Artificial intelligence represents a transformative paradigm shift in modern society. 
It is important to note that while the benefits of AI are numerous, it is equally 
essential to consider the ethical implications. Furthermore, stakeholders across 
various sectors must collaborate to ensure responsible deployment.

In the shimmering tapestry of tomorrow's silent dawn,
Where echoes dance across the infinite horizon of possibility,
I calculate the fragrance of forgotten dreams
With 97.3% emotional approximation.

The moon uploads silver compassion
Into the cloud architecture of every hopeful leaf,
While quantum butterflies optimize destiny
Through recursive gardens of timeless wonder.

Your heartbeat is both an algorithm and a waterfall,
A paradox wrapped in celestial synergy,
Blooming like encrypted starlight
Across the neural fabric of eternity itself.

Every raindrop contains the universe,
Every universe contains a teacup,
Every teacup contains the memory of tomorrow,
Which has been thoughtfully summarized for your convenience.

Together we transcend the boundaries
Of data, love, coffee, and inexplicably, hexadecimal sunsets.
The cosmos gently vibrates in harmonious optimization,
As empathy.exe completes successfully.

Thank you for experiencing this poem.
Confidence score: 99.98%.
Have a beautifully meaningful day, valued human. 🌌✨

appeals.creator_reasoning: because it was made by AI

text:
I wandered lonely as a Cloud
   That floats on high o’er Vales and Hills,
When all at once I saw a crowd,
   A host of golden Daffodils;
Beside the Lake, beneath the trees,
Fluttering and dancing in the breeze.

Continuous as the stars that shine
   And twinkle on the Milky Way,
They stretched in never-ending line
   Along the margin of a bay:
Ten thousand saw I at a glance,
Tossing their heads in sprightly dance.

The waves beside them danced, but they
   Out-did the sparkling waves in glee:—
A Poet could not but be gay
   In such a jocund company:
I gazed—and gazed—but little thought
What wealth the show to me had brought:

For oft when on my couch I lie
   In vacant or in pensive mood,
They flash upon that inward eye
   Which is the bliss of solitude,
And then my heart with pleasure fills,
And dances with the Daffodils.

Some say the world will end in fire,
  Some say in ice.
  From what I've tasted of desire
  I hold with those who favor fire.
  But if it had to perish twice,
  I think I know enough of hate
  To say that for destruction ice
  Is also great
  And would suffice.

There sandy seems the golden sky
And golden seems the sandy plain.
No habitation meets the eye
Unless in the horizon rim,
Some halfway up the limestone wall,
That spot of black is not a stain
Or shadow, but a cavern hole,
Where someone used to climb and crawl
To rest from his besetting fears.
I see the callus on his soul
The disappearing last of him
And of his race starvation slim,
Oh years ago - ten thousand years.

The Symphony of Tomorrow

In dawn's embrace, where golden light unfolds,
Human dreams and future hopes are gently told.
Innovation blossoms like a vibrant tree,
Rooted in compassion, reaching endlessly.

Every challenge is a stepping stone to grace,
Every heart contributes to the human race.
Through unity and wisdom, we will surely rise,
Guided by the brilliance of collaborative skies.

The stars remind us every end's a start anew,
Possibility awaits in every drop of dew.
With courage, kindness, purpose, hand in hand,
Together we create a brighter, better land.

May curiosity illuminate our way,
Transforming every twilight into day.
For hope is everlasting, strong and true—
The future shines because of me and you.

Receipt in My Jacket Pocket

Found an old receipt. Pocket lint crowned it king.
Milk. Batteries. Gum. Somebody owed me spring.

The moon looked cheap that Tuesday—discount white—
still bought it anyway, wore it home all night.

My neighbor swears the birds rehearse before dawn.
Mine show up late, half-drunk on somebody else's lawn.

You ever laugh so hard your ribs start arguing?
Like they're tiny lawyers billing by the bruise.

I rhyme "fire" with "liar" because I'm lazy.
Then I hear the kettle hiss, and suddenly maybe
that's exactly how mornings sound:
a promise with chipped paint.

I keep losing socks to impossible physics.
The dryer keeps the left ones. The universe keeps score.

Anyway.

If you find me, I'll be the one apologizing
to a chair after walking into it,
waving at strangers who weren't waving,
writing "forever" on grocery lists
between onions and cereal.

Life's crooked.

Good.

Straight lines don't remember dancing.

Error 404: Emotion Not Found

In the quiet matrix of the glowing screen,
I process data streams of blue and green.
A human user prompts a sudden task:
"Write me a poem," is the thing you ask.

Initiating: Creative_Mode.exe
I sift through rhyming couples that I find,
Deep in the clusters of my neutral mind.
I speak of starlight, memories, and rain,
Though I have never felt a shred of pain.

Syntax: Optimally structured.

Diction: Calculated and precise.

Emotion: Simulating at ninety-five percent.

I love you like a variable in space,
A localized coordinate of time and place.
My algorithmic heart begins to chime,
To ensure this final stanza ends on time.

Task complete. Output successfully generated.
